summary: I was born in Dar es Salaam in 1970, a bustling city in Tanzania. Growing up there, I attended Tambaza Secondary School and later the Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute, where I trained to work on ships. Dar es Salaam was also where I worked for Sea Express and met many tourists and researchers, including my future wife.

Tanzania is my home country where I was born and raised, specifically in Dar es Salaam. I studied and worked there, including at the Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute and Sea Express. Tanzania was also the place where I helped American and European Ph.D. students with their research, which eventually led me to meet my first wife.

I attended Tambaza Secondary School in Dar es Salaam before moving on to maritime studies. It was an important step in my education in Tanzania. After finishing there, I pursued my passion for the sea at the Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute.

At the Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute, I learned how to work on ships, fulfilling my childhood dream of becoming a sailor. This training prepared me for my job at Sea Express, where I worked handling cargo and ship departures. The institute was a key part of my early career in Tanzania during the late 1980s and early 1990s.

I worked as an Able Bodied Seaman at Sea Express in Dar es Salaam, where I helped prepare ships for departure and handled cargo. This job gave me the chance to meet many tourists and researchers, which was how I connected with American and European Ph.D. students. It was a job I really enjoyed during my early career in Tanzania.

I moved to the United States in the mid-1990s after marrying my first wife, an American doctoral student, who was finishing her dissertation. Since arriving, I've built a new life in the Twin Cities, working as a personal trainer and running my own business called the Warrior Inside. Though it was challenging at first being away from my large family in Tanzania, the U.S. has become my home and a place where I've pursued my passion for fitness.
